audits.excerpt: "It is narrower now. Less a pull. More a line."
audits.reason: Jaina's update is still delivered as pure signal-language. The moment does not route the change through throat, teeth, fingers, bread, or any other resisting surface, so the grounding slips back into abstract magical telemetry.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep the bread in play and make the change hit her body before the sentence: let her thumb crush the crust, swallow against the stale sweetness in her mouth, and then say the pull has tightened into one line inland.

audits.reason: The match between the visible spoor and Jaina's magical read is asserted instead of demonstrated. The prose needs one immediate bodily or environmental confirmation so the overlap does not remain schematic.
audits.suggestedFix: Add a local proof beat before or during the line—her gaze locking on the sled groove, her fingers twitching toward the same cut in the snow, or the bread going still in her hand as the two tracks align.

audits.excerpt: "It is louder," she said. ... "Closer." ... "The plague always carried rot in it. This doesn't blur through the air. It repeats."
audits.reason: This is still explanatory systems-talk. 'Louder,' 'closer,' and 'repeats' name pattern without enough sensory friction, so the read drifts away from embodied perception into analytical summary.
audits.suggestedFix: Translate the explanation into body-carrier and resistance: the wrong taste rising behind her teeth, the rhythm hitting her in pulses between breaths, and the flask seam biting her thumb while she forces out the comparison.

audits.excerpt: "The line bends. We're not following a broad spread anymore. We're approaching one source."
audits.reason: The route update remains abstract cartography. The chapter needs the bend to register through visible terrain or the way Jaina physically turns toward it, not just through disembodied terminology.
audits.suggestedFix: Have her chin snap toward the bluffs or the track kink around a drift while she speaks, so 'bends' and 'one source' are tied to a concrete turn in ground and body.

audits.excerpt: "Shelter arrived only as insult: a ridge too low to break the wind, boulders too slick with rime to trust, the buried curve of some enormous rib cage showing through the drifts. ... A snapped axle jutted out of a drift. A burst sack of grain lay frozen in a yellow arc beside a rock..."
audits.reason: The march has texture, but Dragonblight still reads too generically winter-hostile for too long. The source material gives this corridor signature dead-zone markers—dragon-bone scale and preserved Lordaeron casualties—and the prose only brushes one rib before reverting to portable hardship.
audits.suggestedFix: Insert one local landmark of unmistakable Dragonblight specificity—a rib taller than a man forcing the line around it, or a frozen Lordaeron body in frosted plate beside the axle—so the theater stops feeling swappable.

audits.excerpt: "The plague in Lordaeron repeated itself under the surface. Flesh was easier to read than the machinery beneath it. Same turns. Same ordering. Same answer buried under different ruin. This does too."
audits.reason: The Frostmourne briefing slips into theory-summary. Even in a briefing scene, this much clean conceptual language in a row needs harder attachment to the hot cup, the tabletop, her fingers, or Muradin's visible reaction.
audits.suggestedFix: Break the explanation against the room: let her fingertip drag through spilled ale on the table or tap the grain with each step of the comparison, then cut the phrasing down to the few words she can get out before breath and pain catch up.

audits.excerpt: "I've watched companies drill for twenty years and seen less trust than the two of ye spend on a turn of the head. She speaks, ye move. Ye move, she changes the ground under it. No wasted shouting. No one proving who's cleverest while arrows are flying."
audits.reason: Muradin's read becomes generalized praise instead of staying rooted in what he can literally point to. The speech is accurate, but it floats above cups, stance, table edges, and remembered movement longer than it should.
audits.suggestedFix: Make him pin the evaluation to visible specifics in the room—Jaina still on the cup for heat, Sylvanas already standing to leave, the rangers shifting when either woman glances—so the compliment comes out of observation rather than commentary.

audits.excerpt: "I hear it," she said. ... Even to Sylvanas the cave was wrong. Colder and cleaner than danger. ... A ward lay across the mouth and pushed living flesh back.
audits.reason: The threshold is stronger than a typical abstract read, but it still leans on generalized wrongness. The ward needs one more contact-level manifestation so the barrier feels encountered, not merely diagnosed.
audits.suggestedFix: Add a local physical refusal at the mouth—breath snapping thin, bow leather going rigid, the skin over Sylvanas's knuckles tightening, or Jaina's wrist trembling harder as the barrier presses back.

audits.excerpt: "They're inside the field," Jaina said, too fast. "Don't let them bracket us. And don't cast across the center lane unless I tell you."
audits.reason: This is excellent tactical urgency, but the first clause still depends on invisible jargon. One beat of visible evidence should precede it so 'inside the field' is readable as an observed condition, not just mage terminology.
audits.suggestedFix: Preload the line with a concrete tell—the soldiers turning together, frost webbing over their pauldrons, or spell-light dying short of their boots—then let Jaina name the consequence.

audits.excerpt: "There." ... "Still measuring. Her fingers changed shape in motion, writing over the failed cast, mapping where the field had caught and turned it."
audits.reason: The prose slips into process language at the exact point the fight should stay tactile. 'Measuring,' 'mapping,' and 'field' summarize analysis instead of making the reader watch her do it in space.
audits.suggestedFix: Replace the summary with a visible recalibration—two fingers slicing a tighter angle through the shard-fall, her hand tracking the exact place the spell burst sideways, frost needling her knuckles while she redraws it.

audits.excerpt: "He crossed the ground with impossible speed."
audits.reason: 'Impossible speed' is portable emphasis, not grounded action. The attack wants one concrete movement detail—traction, spray, reach, impact preparation—so the acceleration lives in the chamber rather than in narrator verdict.
audits.suggestedFix: Replace the phrase with a physical lane read: his sabatons shaving frost off the floor, one stride eating the blue light between them, the sword low enough to score sparks or ice dust before Muradin meets it.

audits.excerpt: "Jaina had read it too. One hand locked around her own wrist to steady it. The other stayed out toward the killing lane; she kept trying to finish the pattern as the field shifted under her hand."
audits.reason: The fight-state is clear, but 'finish the pattern' and 'field shifted' still abstract the moment. The scene needs the pattern to show itself through fingerwork, air marks, or collision with the space between her and Arthas.
audits.suggestedFix: Show the unfinished work in concrete fragments—two hooked motions that keep dying short, frost snapping from her fingertips, or the same angle failing against the air in front of her before Sylvanas calls the question.

audits.excerpt: "He had already measured the exchange and found no threat worth haste on their terms."
audits.reason: This is tactical summary after the prose has already given better physical evidence. The sentence collapses visible menace back into essayed conclusion.
audits.suggestedFix: Swap the summary for a material read—his stance settling, Frostmourne held easy, no lunge at the gap, only the certainty of a man who knows the narrowed exit is beneath his concern.

audits.excerpt: "She stopped thinking prince. She had come north to catch a prince in time. The creature in the cave had worn the prince's face and voice, but those were scraps now."
audits.reason: The realization is thematically correct but delivered as retrospective summary. After a physically intense retreat, the turn wants to stay attached to the carried weight, the cave behind them, or the last image of Arthas on the ice.
audits.suggestedFix: Anchor the recognition to one fresh concrete remnant—his voice emptied of heat, the arrow ringing on stone, or Muradin's blood freezing around the shard—then let Sylvanas make the mental severing through that image.

audits.excerpt: "I heard the seam in it," she said. ... "A seam. Spells hit it and turn the same way every time."
audits.reason: The key payoff still arrives in diagnostic language. Because 'seam' matters later, this chapter needs the first articulation to feel more discovered in the body and less delivered as neat analytical shorthand.
audits.suggestedFix: Keep 'seam' but add the carrier: she says it while rubbing numb fingers together, hearing the same hitch in the pattern with each step, or describing the place where the blade 'breathes wrong' before tightening it into the word.

audits.excerpt: "She would not have changed the route, the spacing, the timing, or the partner beside her."
audits.reason: This is clean thematic closure, but it reads like post-action evaluation rather than lived aftershock. The line needs some residue from breath, pain, or the carry to keep it from flattening into summary judgment.
audits.suggestedFix: Let the conviction rise through immediate pressure—Muradin's harness cutting into palms, lungs burning on the descent, Jaina still drawing beside her—then compress the statement into a harder, more bodily refusal to blame the plan or the woman next to her.

audits.excerpt: "To the right of the mouth, old warning runes had been cut into blue ice and half-scoured by drift."
audits.reason: The runes are present, but only as scenic label. This is a source-fidelity miss: the warning never becomes actionable threshold evidence, so the inscription risks reading decorative instead of consequential.
audits.suggestedFix: Give the runes one local interaction—Muradin knocking snow off the grooves, Jaina flinching at the carved line, or Sylvanas catching a single legible warning word through the ice—before the party commits.
